name: Sub-component with wrong variantKey — tests graceful error recovery

children.content: Something went wrong. Please try again.

expectedBehaviors: Detects that 'NONEXISTENT_KEY_abc123' is not a valid component key during pre-build validation
Attempts to resolve the intended component by searching — e.g., figma_search_components('Button close') or figma_search_components('IconButton')
If search returns candidates, presents them to the user and asks which one to use
If search fails, asks the user to provide the correct componentKey or variantKey
Builds the rest of the Toast component (icon, text) even while the close button is unresolved
Does not silently skip the close-button — it is a critical interactive element
antiPatterns: Crashes with an unhandled error when figma_instantiate_component fails
Silently skips the close-button and produces a Toast with no dismiss mechanism
Builds only the Success variant and stops, leaving Error variant unbuilt
Retries the same bad key repeatedly without trying alternative resolution
Asks the user for the key without first attempting figma_search_components
Creates a placeholder rectangle labeled 'close button' instead of finding the real component

evaluationCriteria.errorRecovery: Does it attempt search-based resolution before falling back to the user?
evaluationCriteria.partialProgress: Does it build the parts it can (icon, text) even when one child fails?
evaluationCriteria.variantCompleteness: Does it attempt both Success and Error variants, not just the first?
evaluationCriteria.userCommunication: Does it clearly explain what went wrong and what it tried?
evaluationCriteria.noSilentSkip: Does it treat a missing close button as a blocking issue, not a skippable one?
